name: Pinehurst No. 2
designer: Donald Ross; restored by Coore & Crenshaw (2011)
green_fee_notes: No. 2 is sold through Pinehurst Resort stay-and-play packages (two-night minimum) rather than a rack rate; adding it to a package carries a $250 surcharge and peak rounds are generally reported at $500-650, caddie not included.
notable: Donald Ross's masterpiece (opened 1907), restored by Coore & Crenshaw and famous for its turtleback greens
Has hosted U.S. Opens in 1999 (Payne Stewart), 2005, 2014 and 2024, and is the USGA's first U.S. Open anchor site
Hosted back-to-back U.S. Open and U.S. Women's Open championships in the same summer (2014)
Plays as par 70 for the U.S. Open (holes 8 and 16 convert from par 5 to par 4); resort par is 72

confidence.course_rating: USGA NCRDB (CourseID 23725) rates all tees against par 72; U.S. Open tees 77.9/149. Ross and Tufts combo tees (73.6/141 and 70.3/136) omitted from the card, no per-hole breakdown printed.
confidence.green_fee_notes: Pinehurst publishes no rack rate for No. 2; figures from golf.com reporting and greenfeetracker.com's 2026 range.
confidence.lat_lng: Wikipedia's coordinates for the Pinehurst Resort clubhouse; the No. 2 routing spreads south of this point.
confidence.handicap_per_hole: Men's (Blue/White) allocation from the official 2026 scorecard; the Green/Red row differs.
